_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Persistent ear discharge due to tympanic membrane perforation with conductive hearing loss.
Tympanic perforation without hearing loss or with sensorineural hearing loss may be considered for repair in the presence of need for water sports activity or hearing aid usage.

preauth.label: Clinical notes (detailing signs, symptoms, ear examination findings, indications for doing the procedure & advice for admission)

claim.label: Intra-operative photograph with time and date (optional)
Invoice of the ossicular prosthesis used, if any

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes (including clinical symptoms such as decreased hearing, otalgia, perforation of tympanic membrane, etc., indications for doing the procedure & advise for admission)?
Does the patient complaint of ear discharge and functional hearing loss?

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of the surgery?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advice including for follow-up?
Is the invoice of graft used?

common_queries: Patient does not complain of ear discharge and functional hearing loss, or this is not documented.
Clinical notes for pre-authorization are missing details on signs, symptoms, ear examination findings, indications, or advice for admission.
Audiogram report is missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Operative notes are missing or fail to detail surgical steps and outcomes of the surgery.
Invoice for ossicular prosthesis used is missing.
Documents showing appropriate post-op care or follow-up advice are not available.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Tympanoplasty Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, and ear examination.
Tympanoplasty is a surgical technique to repair a defect in the tympanic membrane by placement of a graft using different techniques with or without ossicular reconstruction.
contraindications: Surgery in the only hearing ear needs caution.
etiology: Infection of the middle ear - chronic suppurative otitis media (most common cause)
barotrauma (pressure exerted on ear drum associated with air travel)
following ear surgeries like tympanostomy tube placement
risk_factors: Adults & children both but more common in children
investigations: Pure tone audiometry to measure air bone gap (ABG) (i.e. the difference between air conduction threshold and bone conduction threshold) before and after surgery
complications: cholesteatoma development (skin disease/ cyst in the ear)
persistence or worsening of any conductive hearing loss
